title-group.article-title: Quaternion Lifting for Multichannel RF and Sensing Signals
title-group.subtitle: A Canonical Lane Representation for Quaternionic Signal Processing

abstract.p: This paper defines a canonical quaternion lifting procedure for multichannel RF and sensing signals whose external intake remains standard complex-channel data. Given two complex channels z1[n]=a[n]+ib[n] and z2[n]=c[n]+id[n], the lift forms a quaternion lane q[n]=z1[n]+z2[n]j = a[n]+b[n]i+c[n]j+d[n]k. For larger captures, channels are grouped deterministically into quaternion lanes Q[g,n] in H. The paper formalizes this lift, its inverse unpacking map, odd-channel handling, and representation-level invariants that make it suitable as a canonical internal form.

license.license-p: This is an open-access article distributed under the terms of the Creative Commons Attribution License (CC BY 4.0).

sec.title: Introduction
sec.p: The manuscript introduces a deterministic grouped quaternion lift as a representation layer for ordered multichannel complex captures while preserving the external complex-channel input contract.

sec.title: Scope and external signal model
sec.p: An ordered capture Z=(z_c[n]) with C complex channels and N samples per channel is mapped internally into grouped quaternion lanes without changing caller-facing channel format.

sec.title: Quaternion preliminaries
sec.p: The paper adopts standard quaternion coordinates in H with basis units i, j, k and uses coordinate-level algebra only, deferring transform conventions to later papers.

sec.title: Canonical quaternion lift
sec.p: For two channels, q[n]=z_1[n]+z_2[n]j. For larger ordered captures, channels are deterministically grouped into lanes Q[g,n] with an explicit odd-tail rule.

sec.title: Recoverability and representation invariants
sec.p: The paper proves exact inversion by an unpacking map, determinism under fixed ordering, and energy preservation from complex-channel totals to grouped quaternion-lane totals.

sec.title: Why the lift matters for RF and sensing
sec.p: The representation preserves paired channel structure for dual-polarization, paired MIMO streams, phased-array/radar-adjacent sensing, and reproducible archived replay analysis.

sec.title: Downstream role in quaternionic signal processing
sec.p: The lift is positioned as the representation layer that supports later quaternionic spectral, coherence, and orientation-aware processing stages.

sec.title: Scope, limitations, and non-claims
sec.p: The manuscript does not claim new sensing physics, universal hardware advantage, or benchmark improvements from lifting alone.

sec.title: Conclusion
sec.p: The canonical grouped lift is a deterministic, lossless, and energy-preserving internal form for ordered multichannel complex captures.

element-citation.source: Quaternions and Rotation Sequences: A Primer with Applications to Orbits, Aerospace, and Virtual Reality

element-citation.article-title: Singular Value Decomposition of Quaternion Matrices: A New Tool for Vector-Sensor Signal Processing
Hypercomplex Fourier Transforms of Color Images
